summary.intent: Implement GitHub issues #245 (ContextMenu agnostic core) and #249 (MenuBar agnostic core) for the ars-ui project in a single combined PR, following the repo's TDD and post-implementation audit workflow.
summary.outcome: Both agnostic core components were fully implemented with state machines, ARIA attrs, dispatch helpers, per-part insta snapshots, spec-conformance tests, proptest invariants, and updated component/adapter specs. The commit was created and passed the full `cargo xci-fast` gate, but the PR push and opening were the final pending step at the end of the transcript.

repo: Snapshot fixtures follow a per-part/per-branch pattern (one `.snap` file per named assertion), not one aggregate snapshot per component — matches `crates/ars-components/src/selection/menu/snapshots/`.
Agnostic core machines use `NoEffect` as their effect type; live DOM focus, floating positioning, timers, and outside-click listeners are strictly adapter-owned.
Post-implementation audit is a mandatory workflow step with spec/code drift checking and at least two 'anything else missing?' rounds before the PR is opened.
GitHub Project board items must be moved to 'In Progress' before coding begins (project ID 2, `ars-ui implementation roadmap`).
`cargo mutants` is a required quality gate with a 0-missed-mutants target; surviving mutants must be killed with tests or justified as equivalent mutations in `.cargo/mutants.toml`.
`cargo xci-fast` is the required pre-push gate and must pass before any commit.

Spec files for both agnostic cores and Leptos/Dioxus adapters live under `spec/components/selection/`, `spec/leptos-components/selection/`, and `spec/dioxus-components/selection/`, and spec changes must ship in the same PR as implementation.
`cargo xtask spec validate`, `cargo xtask spec toc <file>`, and `cargo xtask spec compile-snippets` are all part of the required spec validation workflow.

code.finding: Pre-existing bug: the highlight proptest utility module was loaded unconditionally, but all its helpers require the `i18n` feature. Without that feature gate on the module, `cargo mutants` (which runs without `--features i18n`) hit a baseline compile failure. Fixed by gating the module with `#[cfg(feature = "i18n")]`.

The `not(feature = "std")` timestamp fallback path cannot be observed by the default std-enabled mutation profile, so a narrow `.cargo/mutants.toml` exclusion was added for that specific region rather than writing a test that would never run in CI.

The `adjacent_key` helper had a type mismatch on first compile: collection neighbor lookups return borrowed keys while the empty-focus branch returned owned keys. Normalizing all arms to return owned `Key` fixed it.
The existing `Menu` snapshot test block is the canonical pattern to follow for per-part snapshot assertions in new selection components.

workflow: Running `cargo insta test --unreferenced=delete` without a precise test filter will delete unrelated snapshot fixtures — always scope the filter tightly or use `--unreferenced=reject` for verification and accept/delete manually.

`cargo mutants` first run on a fresh module typically surfaces 30–40% missed mutants concentrated in builder setters, callback guards, stale-reference cleanup, and disabled/focusable item predicates — plan a second test-writing pass.

When `cargo mutants` baseline fails due to feature-gated code, the root cause is usually an unconditional `mod` declaration pulling in feature-gated helpers rather than a problem in the new module being tested.

Coverage per-module with `cargo llvm-cov --summary-only --json` plus `jq` filtering on `.data[0].files[].filename` is the reliable way to extract per-file line/region percentages from a focused test run.
The co-author trailer in commit messages should use `Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>` for AI-assisted work in this repo.

friction: Initial compile was slow due to a fresh-ish `target/` directory; first red-test run took several minutes before producing output.

`cargo mutants` had a silent baseline compile failure (no error banner, just no mutant output) caused by the pre-existing `i18n`-ungated utility highlight module — diagnosis required cross-referencing the proptest harness entrypoints manually.
Two full mutation passes were needed for context_menu (195 → 191 candidates after exclude, 71 → 0 missed) requiring a substantial second test-writing round before the gate was clean.

`cargo insta test --unreferenced=delete` ran too broadly and deleted unrelated snapshots; required `git restore --staged :/` and a restore pass to recover them before re-accepting only the new fixtures.
The `cargo llvm-cov` `--include-filename-regex` flag did not work as expected for filtering the report to a single module; had to fall back to generating a JSON summary and filtering with `jq`.

The `cargo xci-fast` gate ran through 10 steps including trybuild contract checks and took a substantial amount of time, with no streaming progress indicator for individual test binaries during the integration stage.
open_items: Leptos and Dioxus adapter components for ContextMenu and MenuBar are explicitly out of scope for this PR and need separate issues/implementation.
PR push and opening against `fogodev/ars-ui main` with `Closes #245` / `Closes #249` keywords were the final pending steps at the end of the transcript — not yet done.
The waiting-for-codex-review loop (`.agents/skills/waiting-for-codex-review/SKILL.md`) needs to be invoked after the PR is opened to start the `@codex review` / address-threads / re-trigger cycle.
The `snapshot-reviewed` label (`gh pr edit <pr> --add-label snapshot-reviewed`) needs to be applied to the PR since new `.snap` fixtures were added.
Issues #245 and #249 still show `In Progress` on the project board; they will close automatically only once the PR with the auto-close keywords is merged.
